methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $10M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $110M - $200M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
General Galactic is a Seed-stage climate and energy company founded by Halen Mattison and Luke Neise that has raised $10M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer universe of 9 private companies and 1 public comparable (LNZA) operating in the same sector, including adjacent-stage reference points such as Graphyte, Fourth Power, and Steady Energy.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-Progression analysis applies a typical 2.5x multiplier to the $10M raised, implying a $25M valuation. Peer-Median analysis benchmarks against three same-sector Series A peers with a median valuation of $150M (range: $110M–$200M). The disparity reflects the stage and maturity gap between Seed and Series A entrants in the climate-tech space.

The triangulated analysis implies a range of $21M (low) to $172M (high), with a median of $88M. The wide disparity between methods underscores material uncertainty: Stage-Progression anchors conservatively to capital deployed, while Peer-Median reflects valuations of more advanced-stage competitors. A Seed valuation for General Galactic in the $25M–$60M band aligns more closely with Stage-Progression logic and de-risks peer multiples. Key uncertainties include proof-of-concept progress, path to Series A capital deployment, and competitive positioning within the crowded climate-energy frontier-tech segment.
